channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Harris gives Democrats a boost, but control of House up for grabs
item.description: Democrats have a fair shot at flipping control of the House — largely because of the boost provided by Vice President Harris at the top of the ticket — but the battle is a horse race that is too close to call heading into the final weeks of the campaign, according to a host of
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation

item.title: Bath and Body Works apologizes for candle designed with what looked like KKK hoods
item.description: Bath and Body Works apologized for selling a winter-themed candle that came under scrutiny for label imagery that drew comparisons to Ku Klux Klan hoods, according to multiple reports.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Group associated with polygamous sect builds fences on US Forest Service lands
item.description: A group in Colorado has fenced a parcel of U.S. Forest Service Land in an attempt to claim the land under the Homestead Act of 1862.
item.dc:creator: Heather Willard, KREX 5

item.title: Voters with disabilities are feeling ignored by presidential candidates
item.description: A new report from Rutgers University estimates that about 40.2 million eligible voters in the quickly approaching U.S. presidential election are disabled.
item.dc:creator: KENYA HUNTER and AYANNA ALEXANDER, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Israeli strike in northern Lebanon kills at least 18 people
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — An Israeli airstrike hit an apartment building in northern Lebanon on Monday, killing at least 18 people, according to the Lebanese Red Cross. There was no immediate comment from the Israeli military and it was not clear what the target was.
item.dc:creator: KAREEM CHEHAYEB, WAFAA SHURAFA and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Things to know about AP's report on the federal criminal cases against Donald Trump
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A year that began with the prospect of a federal court reckoning for Donald Trump will conclude without any chance at trial, leaving voters without the finality of an up-or-down jury verdict in the two most consequential cases against the Republican presidential nominee.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER and ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Influx of homeless encampments under political signages seen across Las Vegas valley
item.description: Seeing political signage on almost every corner is nothing new during an election year, but what’s even more noticeable is the influx of unhoused people making shelter underneath.
item.dc:creator: Madison Kimbro, ABC 4

item.title: WATCH: NASA launches Europa Clipper, will scour Jupiter moon for ingredients of life
item.description: Coverage of the launch is expected to begin at 11 a.m. ET and will be added to this story in the video player above. The launch window itself is set to open at 12:06 p.m. ET.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press via Nexstar Media Wire, KRON4

item.title: Polish government's decision to suspend asylum law triggers concerns from civil society
item.description: WARSAW, Poland (AP) — Human rights and civil society groups voiced concerns Monday after Polish Prime Minister Donald Tusk announced a plan to temporarily suspend the right to asylum because of its alleged abuse by eastern neighbors Belarus and Russia.
item.dc:creator: VANESSA GERA, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Trump suggests using National Guard, military against 'enemy from within'
item.description: Former President Donald Trump in a Sunday interview suggested using the National Guard or the military on Election Day to combat what he described as potential chaos from “the enemy from within” – a group that Trump said includes “radical left lunatics.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, KRON4

item.title: Jerry West becomes first three-time inductee as Carter and Billups enter basketball's Hall of Fame
item.description: SPRINGFIELD, Mass. (AP) — The stage was filled with Laker greats when Jerry West made history Sunday night by becoming the first three-time inductee to the Naismith Memorial Basketball Hall of Fame.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN MAHONEY, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Eggnog before Thanksgiving? Shoppers scramble store calendars
item.description: Holiday merchandise traditionally started showing up in stores mid-October, and holiday offerings ramped up starting mid-November.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Pilot's wife safely lands in California after her husband suffers medical emergency during flight
item.description: The wife of a pilot who suffered a medical emergency during a flight took over flying and was able to safely land the aircraft in California.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israel says 4 soldiers killed by Hezbollah drone attack while Israeli strike in Gaza leaves 20 dead
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — A Hezbollah drone attack on an army base in central Israel killed four soldiers and severely wounded seven others Sunday, the military said, in the deadliest strike by the militant group since Israel launched its ground invasion of Lebanon nearly two weeks ago.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Man with loaded gun arrested at checkpoint near Donald Trump's weekend rally in Southern California
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — A Nevada man with a shotgun, loaded handgun, ammunition and several fake passports in his vehicle was arrested at a security checkpoint outside Donald Trump's rally Saturday night in the Southern California desert, authorities said. He was released the same day on $5,000 bail.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTOPHER WEBER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Matt McCarty goes from Korn Ferry to the Masters after winning in Utah for first PGA Tour title
item.description: Matt McCarty started the year on the Korn Ferry Tour and now is headed to the Masters, winning his first PGA Tour title Sunday at the Black Desert Championship with a 4-under 67 and one swing that all but sealed it.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: What $35 trillion in US national debt means for Americans
item.description: With the national debt standing at more than $35 trillion, economists fear that the country's debt load will only grow.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, WGN 9

item.title: Rare comet spotted over Southwest Virginia
item.description: According to a blog post by NASA, the comet is named ATLAS and was first discovered in 2023 as its orbit approached the inner solar system.
item.dc:creator: Faith Little, NewsNation

item.title: Missing non-verbal 5-year-old boy in Waldorf found dead in pond
item.description: CHARLES COUNTY, Md. (DC News Now) -- After hours of searching for a missing 5-year-old boy with autism in Charles County, the Charles County Sheriff's Office (CCSO) said he was found dead inside a pond. According to CCSO, at 11 a.m. on Sunday, officers responded to the 1000 block of St.
item.dc:creator: Jenny Gable, NewsNation

item.title: From the pulpit, Harris calls out Trump for hurricane misinformation. Biden surveys Florida's damage
item.description: GREENVILLE, N.C. (AP) — Kamala Harris used an appearance Sunday before a largely Black church audience in battleground North Carolina to call out Donald Trump for spreading misinformation about the government's hurricane response.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK, AAMER MADHANI and DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Mets add McNeil to NLCS roster while Dodgers drop Vesia and Rojas, add Kiermaier and Honeywell
item.description: Jeff McNeil was added to the New York Mets' roster for the NL Championship Series against the Los Angeles Dodgers after recovering from a broken right wrist. Right-handed reliever Adam Ottavino was dropped from the Mets' roster. Tommy Edman will start at shortstop throughout the NLCS, Roberts said.
item.dc:creator: BETH HARRIS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Sam's Club opening location in Texas, but there's no checkout lanes
item.description: Sam's Club CEO Chris Nicholas says the warehouse club chain will soon open a store in Texas but there won't be any checkout lanes.
item.dc:creator: Kyler Swaim, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Nearly 200 miles of Blue Ridge Parkway reopens as long-term work remains in North Carolina
item.description: The Blue Ridge Parkway was closed indefinitely after Hurricane Helene swept through the region last month.
item.dc:creator: QCN Digital Staff, Fox 5

item.title: WATCH: Ohio dad dragged under car by 9-year-old, facing criminal charges
item.description: An Ohio dad who was dragged under the car while teaching his son how to drive, is facing criminal charges, according to local reports.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Euthanasia, pronouns, sports betting: Intriguing 2024 ballot measures
item.description: Several states have placed important measures on their ballots, ranging from flag designs to end-of-life care.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Will Donald Trump and Kamala Harris debate again?
item.description: While there was originally talk of more than one meeting between Trump and Harris, the former president made his intentions clear with a post on Truth Social.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, KRON4

item.title: Harris helps pack aid boxes for North Carolina hurricane victims ahead of Sunday campaign push
item.description: Vice President Kamala Harris on Saturday helped pack diapers into boxes of personal care products destined for North Carolina hurricane victims, agreeing with one helper who said “it takes a village.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Florida Gov. DeSantis helps Waffle House employees cook for first responders
item.description: Florida Gov. Ron DeSantis put on the gloves and picked up a spatula at a Sarasota-area Waffle House on Thursday to help cook for first responders after Hurricane Milton left a path of destruction across the Tampa Bay area.
item.dc:creator: Kevin Accettulla, NewsNation

item.title: Doritos to open its first-ever restaurant
item.description: It’s not just chips that will be available; the location will serve everything from margaritas and veggie dumplings to ice cream and California rolls, all with a Doritos twist.
item.dc:creator: Will Conybeare, WGN 9

item.title: Rising chocolate prices impact Halloween candy choices
item.description: Despite rising chocolate and candy prices, Americans are expected to spend $3.5 million on Halloween sweets this year.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, ABC 4

item.title: Which states have the highest and lowest voter turnout in presidential elections?
item.description: The rate of voter turnout for the 2020 presidential election was around 67%. But some states were really bringing up that average.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KTLA 5

item.title: Obama’s callout to Black men touches a nerve among Democrats. Is election-year misogyny at play?
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Barack Obama had frank words for Black men who may be considering sitting out the election.
item.dc:creator: MATT BROWN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: CDC: Whooping cough cases surge to record levels in US in 2024
item.description: Whooping cough cases have increased to record levels since 2014, with four times as many cases compared to last year, data from the CDC showed. There have been more than 16,000 cases this year alone and two confirmed deaths, statistics revealed.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, KTLA 5

item.title: UCLA professor says he’s homeless due to low pay
item.description: (KTLA) -- In a now-viral TikTok video, Dr. Daniel McKeown, a professor at UCLA, claims low pay has left him homeless, shocking many of his followers. "Hi everyone, my name is Daniel, and I’m an astrophysics professor at UCLA.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Lewis, Fox 5

item.title: Residents slog through flooded streets, clear debris after Hurricane Milton tore through Florida
item.description: LITHIA, Fla. (AP) — Florida residents slogged through flooded streets, gathered up scattered debris and assessed damage to their homes on Friday after Hurricane Milton smashed through coastal communities and spawned a barrage of deadly tornadoes.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS O'MEARA, BRENDAN FARRINGTON and TY O'NEIL, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: North Carolina governor blasts 'flat out lie' from Trump on disaster aid
item.description: "Trump’s lies and conspiracy theories have hurt the morale of first responders and people who lost everything, helped scam artists and put government and rescue workers in danger," said North Carolina Gov. Roy Cooper.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, Fox 5

item.title: Nobel Peace Prize awarded to Japanese organization of atomic bombing survivors Nihon Hidankyo
item.description: Jørgen Watne Frydnes, chair of the Norwegian Nobel Committee, said the award was made as the “taboo against the use of nuclear weapon is under pressure.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9
